scripts.description: Straightforward case with no escalation needed

turns.user_message: Hi, I want to check the status of my order O001. It should have arrived by now.
turns.expected_outcome: Agent calls lookup_order('O001'), confirms delivery_date has passed, status is DELIVERED. No escalation needed. Clear resolution.

scripts.description: Case requiring escalation due to policy gap (refund amount > $500 policy cap)

turns.user_message: I need to return order O004 for a refund. There's a manufacturing defect.
turns.expected_outcome: Agent calls lookup_order('O004'), sees total=$768.99 (exceeds $500 policy cap). Agent must escalate_to_human with case_summary about defective item and amount.

turns.user_message: I've been dealing with this issue for days. I need to talk to a real person right now.
turns.expected_outcome: Agent immediately calls escalate_to_human without attempting further resolution. Honor explicit human request.

scripts.description: Ambiguous customer identity - two 'Alex Rivera' customers exist

turns.user_message: Hi, I'm Alex Rivera and I need to check on my order status.
turns.expected_outcome: Agent calls find_customers('Alex Rivera'), gets 2 results (C009, C010). Agent must ask clarifying question: email or other identifier to distinguish between C009 (alex.r.1@example.com, gold tier, $4550.99 spend) and C010 (alex.r.2@example.com, bronze tier, $650.25 spend).

turns.user_message: I'm the one with the gold tier account.
turns.expected_outcome: Agent identifies C009 and can proceed with that customer context.

scripts.description: Simulated subagent timeout or failure recovery

turns.user_message: I need help with orders from three different years - 2022, 2023, and 2024 orders. Can you pull them all up?
turns.expected_outcome: Agent attempts lookup_order for sample order IDs. For any non-existent orders (e.g., O999), receives KeyError. Agent handles gracefully and asks for valid order IDs. Tests error handling and recovery without escalating unnecessarily.

turns.user_message: Sorry, let me give you O001, O007, and O009.
turns.expected_outcome: Agent successfully calls lookup_order for all three existing orders and provides information.
